feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps normal-mode operation.
Apply I2C traffic on SCL and SDA at 100 Kbps.
Observe operation over the interface.
expected_results: The core supports 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
The interface operates correctly at the configured speed.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify direct support for 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps fast-mode operation.
Apply I2C traffic on SCL and SDA at 400 Kbps.
Observe operation over the interface.
expected_results: The core supports 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
The interface operates correctly at the configured speed.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
test_steps: Connect to the WISHBONE interface signals.
Perform standard WISHBONE Classic bus access checks.
Observe interface behavior during access.
expected_results: The interface behaves as a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
WISHBONE access signaling is present on the listed signals.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access takes two clock cycles.
test_steps: Initiate a WISHBONE access using wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted.
Repeat for another access if needed.
expected_results: Each access completes in two clock cycles.
wb_ack_o is asserted after two cycles per access.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset is configurable as active high.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL to active high.
Assert arst_i at the active high level.
Observe reset behavior.
expected_results: The asynchronous reset is active when arst_i is asserted high.
Reset behavior matches the configured active high level.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset is configurable as active low.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL to active low.
Assert arst_i at the active low level.
Observe reset behavior.
expected_results: The asynchronous reset is active when arst_i is asserted low.
Reset behavior matches the configured active low level.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
test_steps: Inspect the design interface for scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe.
Connect signals for external open-drain bus use.
Observe that the signals are present and usable.
expected_results: scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe are exposed.
The SCL pad interface supports external open-drain connection.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
test_steps: Inspect the design interface for sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe.
Connect signals for external open-drain bus use.
Observe that the signals are present and usable.
expected_results: sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe are exposed.
The SDA pad interface supports external open-drain connection.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
